description: In 2006, Massachusetts passed landmark health care reform under Governor Mitt Romney, aimed at achieving near-universal health insurance coverage through an individual mandate and subsidized plans. The legislation, often called 'Romneycare,' served as a prototype for the federal Affordable Care Act and faced intense debates regarding the role of government vs. the private market.

key_events.description: Governor Mitt Romney officially introduces the reform package to the state legislature.
A bipartisan coalition in the State House signals strong support, easing fears of gridlock.
Business groups, initially skeptical, voice conditional support for the individual mandate.
Governor Romney signs the bill into law, solidifying public approval among moderates.

calibration_notes: This scenario captures the shift from skepticism toward a radical government-mandated health reform to eventual broad-based acceptance, serving as a template for tracking policy adoption in highly polarized environments.
